additional.context:
## Dieses Dokument

- Titel: Joseph Roth
- Signatur: SZ-AAP/W-AA168.4
- Datum: 1939
- Sprache: Englisch
- Objekttyp: Typoskriptdurchschlag
- Umfang: 2 Blatt
- Schreibinstrument: Schwarzes
                                       Durchschlagpapier, Bleistift, schwarze Tinte
- Hand: Stefan Zweig
- Anmerkungen: Später veröffentlicht in Sunday Times, 28. Mai 1939

pages.text:
Joseph R O T H
------ für Sunday Times 1939

In one single week the German emigration has lost
two of its most prominent figures: Ernst Toller in New York and
today Joseph Roth who succumbed to a sudden heart-attack in Paris.
~~~~artistic Although he was perhaps less known in England
than Ernst Toller, Roth's disappearance means perhaps a greater
loss from an artistic point of view. Born 1894 on the Austrian-
Russian frontier and at first only estimated as a most profound
and brilliant journalist, he won a well-merited world-fame by
his novel JOB which was translated into nearly all languages.
It was the story of an average man of today who suffers all the
testings of Job, and it was told with the simplicity of the Bible
and a wonderful warmth of the heart, one of the books which one
never forgets. Some smaller novels followed. The downfall of
the Austrian Empire inspired him with two full-size novels "The
Radetzkymarsch" and "Die Kapuzinergruft", both of which will remain
as unsurpassed pictures of old Austria in the tragic time of its
moral and material dissolution. No artist from Austria has ever
seen his country with a sharper eye and a more passionate heart;
one could call them an epitaph of the Austrian empire.
Joseph Roth was one of the really great writers of
our days; his German prose has always been a model of perfect
style. He worked every page of his books with the fervour of a
pages.notes: Die erste Seite enthält eine handschriftliche Notiz von Stefan Zweig am Kopf des Dokuments. Das Wort 'artistic' ist durchgestrichen.
Rückseite von Blatt 1, kein Text erkennbar.

pages.text:
true poet; like a goldsmith he polished and repolished every
sentence till the rhythm was perfect and the colour brilliant.
His artistic conscience was as inexorable as his heart was
passionate and tender. The younger generation in German looses
with him a great example and his friends a wonderful friend.
